title: Lower firmem pass crashes with incorrect mask bitwidth
body:
Exposed by a crash in firesim, `FirMemLowering` creates incorrect width mask when lowering the `seq.firmem` at the `hw.instance` but the generated module op has the correct width.

`circt-opt --lower-seq-firmem` produces the crash on the following input
```
error: 'hw.instance' op operand type #5 must be 'i3', but got 'i1'
  %m0_mem3 = seq.firmem 0, 1, undefined, port_order : <12 x 42, mask 3>
             ^
 %1 = "hw.instance"(%arg2, %arg1, %arg0, %arg4, %arg3, %0) {argNames = ["RW0_addr", "RW0_en", "RW0_clk", "RW0_wmode", "RW0_wdata", "RW0_wmask"], instanceName = "m0_mem3_ext", moduleName = @m0_mem3_12x42, parameters = [], resultNames = ["RW0_rdata"]} : (i4, i1, !seq.clock, i1, i42, i1) -> i42

```
```
hw.module @Foo(%clk: !seq.clock, %en: i1, %addr: i4, %wdata: i42, %wmode: i1) {
  %m0_mem3 = seq.firmem 0, 1, undefined, port_order : <12 x 42, mask 3>
  %2 = seq.firmem.read_write_port %m0_mem3[%addr] = %wdata if %wmode, clock %clk enable %en : <12 x 42, mask 3>
  comb.xor %2 : i42

}
```
